briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News / TechCrunch* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News / CNBC* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of fintech middleware company Synapse, highlighting the risks of uninsured fintech banking relationships.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News / Workweek* — Fintech innovation is transforming the traditional credit card model with increasingly niche rewards, features, and underwriting approaches.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News / FF News* — Apple's acquisition of open banking credit assessment startup Credit Kudos signals the tech giant's growing ambitions in financial services.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News / Bits About Money* — A deep-dive analysis examines how open banking frameworks are reshaping competitive dynamics in the payments industry.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News / FinLedger* — Canada's federal government signalled a shift away from screen scraping toward a formal open banking framework, setting an early implementation target relevant to ongoing Canadian financial data reform.

- **[Canada's new sovereign wealth fund: What Ottawa can learn from Alberta](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/calgary/bakx-heritage-trust-sovereign-wealth-carney-9.7184255?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — As Ottawa moves to establish a national sovereign wealth fund, analysts say the federal government should study the lessons — both good and bad — from Alberta's Heritage Trust Fund.

- **[Why a salary of $115K isn't enough to purchase a house in some parts of Canada](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/home-affordability-income-9.7181279?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Rising housing costs across Canada mean that even six-figure earners are being priced out of homeownership in many markets, underscoring the urgent need for financial inclusion solutions.

- **[Women's Equity Lab Manitoba closes Fund 3 at $300,000](https://betakit.com/womens-equity-lab-manitoba-closes-fund-3-at-300000/)** — *BetaKit* — Women's Equity Lab Manitoba has closed its third fund at $300,000, continuing its mission to back underrepresented founders across the Canadian Prairies.

- **[PrairiesCan invests $7.9 million in funding to support Co.Labs, local AI firms](https://betakit.com/prairiescan-invests-7-9-million-in-funding-to-support-co-labs-local-ai-firms/)** — *BetaKit* — The federal regional development agency PrairiesCan is injecting $7.9 million into Prairie tech hub Co.Labs and local AI companies, reflecting growing government investment in regional innovation ecosystems.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — This resource cataloguing the darker side of fintech — including predatory practices, surveillance, and financial exclusion — is a useful reference for ecosystem builders and regulators focused on responsible fintech development in Atlantic Canada.

- **[Canada's real workforce crisis isn't AI, it's the entry-level experience gap](https://betakit.com/canadas-real-workforce-crisis-isnt-ai-its-the-entry-level-experience-gap/)** — *BetaKit* — A timely opinion piece arguing that Canada's biggest talent challenge is the shrinking pipeline of entry-level opportunities, a concern directly relevant to growing the fintech talent base in Atlantic Canada.

- **[Former defence minister Harjit Sajjan's new startup lays groundwork for public listing](https://betakit.com/former-defence-minister-harjit-sajjans-new-startup-lays-groundwork-for-public-listing/)** — *BetaKit* — A high-profile Canadian political figure's transition into the startup and public markets space highlights the growing intersection of policy experience and entrepreneurship in Canada's tech ecosystem.
